title: Typed counter sink primitive + caplog test ledger + T1 doc/spirit LOWs cleanup
intent: Replace the ad-hoc logger.warning("telemetry_counter event=...") pattern emitted by T1F/T1BD/T1C/T1E with a single typed counter sink primitive (read-back queryable, monotonically incrementing, thread-safe, in-process). Add caplog tests that pin every distinct counter event name asserted in the T1 invariants ledger. Land the 6 doc/spirit LOWs accumulated across T1 closures so the T1 review record is debt-free before T2 admission. Operator-visible behavior change: counter values become inspectable beyond log-grep; emission semantics unchanged.

asserted_invariants.text: src/observability/counters.py exposes a typed Counter API: increment(name: str, *, labels: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> None and read(name: str, *, labels: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> int. The module-level state is thread-safe (threading.Lock or equivalent). Reading an un-incremented (name, labels) pair returns 0. Negative deltas are rejected with ValueError. Unit test test_counter_increment_read_isolated_per_label_set asserts counters with different label dicts increment independently.

Every existing logger.warning("telemetry_counter event=...") call site in src/venue/polymarket_v2_adapter.py:322,573 (placeholder_envelope_blocked_total, compat_submit_rejected_total), src/state/db.py:110 (db_write_lock_timeout_total), src/state/portfolio.py:1177,1707 (position_loader_field_defaulted_total, position_projection_field_dropped_total), src/state/chain_reconciliation.py:535-664 (cost_basis_chain_mutation_blocked_total × 4 field labels), src/execution/harvester.py:552,560,1733 (harvester_learning_write_blocked_total) calls src.observability.counters.increment(...) with the same event name as 'name' and the field/path qualifier as 'labels'. The original logger.warning line is preserved (or replaced by a single helper that emits both) so log-grep observability is not regressed.

tests/test_counter_caplog_ledger.py exercises the 7 distinct counter event names emitted by T1 (placeholder_envelope_blocked_total, compat_submit_rejected_total, db_write_lock_timeout_total, position_loader_field_defaulted_total, position_projection_field_dropped_total, cost_basis_chain_mutation_blocked_total, harvester_learning_write_blocked_total) and asserts: (a) the typed sink reflects the increment, AND (b) the legacy log line still fires (caplog match on 'telemetry_counter event=<name>'). Each event name has at least one parametrized test case.

src/state/db.py::_db_busy_timeout_s rejects negative ZEUS_DB_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS values with ValueError at parse time (not at connect time). tests/test_sqlite_busy_timeout.py::test_negative_env_rejected_at_parse_time asserts this. Closes T1E C-1 LOW.

scripts/state_census.py removes the ImportError fallback that hardcoded FILL_AUTHORITY_VENUE_CONFIRMED_FULL='venue_confirmed_full' (T1H C-1). On import failure of src.state.portfolio.FILL_AUTHORITY_VENUE_CONFIRMED_FULL, the census exits non-zero with a clear 'authority constant unavailable; refusing to classify' message rather than silently using a duplicate string. tests/test_state_census.py::test_census_fail_closed_on_missing_authority_constant asserts this.

phase.json files_touched lists are reconciled with the actual git diff at close-time for: (a) T1F (line drift 1395-1397 → 1456-1458 in db.py merge surface; T1F C-2 LOW), (b) T1BD lifecycle_events.py listing (T1BD C-2 LOW; either remove from files_touched if the executor never wrote it, or add a 'read-only context' annotation), (c) T1C invariant text tightened to acknowledge the wrapper-only placement is intentional (T1C C-1 LOW; cite the legacy openmeteo training_allowed=0 audit-pair test). Cleanup commits update each phase.json's _planner_notes with a 'closeout_drift_resolution' field naming the LOW resolved.

_planner_notes.distinct_label_combinations_estimated: ~14 (4 chain_reconciliation field labels + 3 harvester reason labels + 2 portfolio field labels + 5 single-label events)
_planner_notes.caplog_ledger_test_count_estimate: ~14 parametrized cases, one per distinct (event_name, label_dict) tuple

loc_estimate_basis.src/observability/counters.py NEW: ~120 LOC (Counter class + thread-safe increment/read + helper emit_typed_counter that emits both typed sink AND legacy log line so existing log-grep continues to work)
loc_estimate_basis.src/state/db.py + chain_reconciliation.p: ~50 LOC (10 call sites × ~5 LOC delta each)
loc_estimate_basis.tests/test_observability_counters.py NEW: ~150 LOC (sink unit tests: thread-safety, label isolation, negative-delta rejection, read-back monotonicity)
loc_estimate_basis.tests/test_counter_caplog_ledger.py NEW: ~250 LOC (14 parametrized cases × ~15 LOC fixture-driven trigger)
loc_estimate_basis.rebuild_all_v2 partial-commit operator n: 0 LOC (already in T1E commit message; T2F adds it to docs/to-do-list/known_gaps.md or equivalent operator-visible doc — coordinator decides exact target)

k0_k1_classification_per_surface.src/observability/counters.py NEW + test: K1 STATE TRUTH (in-process counter ledger; not LIVE BOUNDARY because no SDK contact, but state-truth because read() values gate alerting)
k0_k1_classification_per_surface.call-site replacement in chain_reconcili: K1 STATE TRUTH (the existing emission semantics is being doubled, not replaced; risk is regression of existing log-grep observability)
k0_k1_classification_per_surface.negative-env validation in db.py: K0 STATE TRUTH (db.py is the single connection authority; an over-tight ValueError at parse time could brick connect)
k0_k1_classification_per_surface.census fail-closed: K1 read-only census; failure mode change is observable but no production-DB write surface

_planner_notes.reviewer_set_rationale: K0 STATE TRUTH surface (db.py negative-env validation) requires opus critic + verifier + git-master. No security-reviewer because no LIVE BOUNDARY (no SDK contact, no venue side effect; this matches the doctrine from T1A + T1BD + T1E retrospective: K0 STATE TRUTH does NOT trigger security probe — only K0 LIVE BOUNDARY does, which T2F is not).
_planner_notes.predecessor_dependency: All 7 T1 phases must be landed (they are, per s3 telemetry: T1A af671406, T1F 0468a9ad, T1BD fccbb93c, T1C 72e58e3a, T1E 76ef3796, T1G 3bcd8778, T1H 98483e79). T2F depends on the call sites that those phases introduced; modifying call sites that no longer match the source would error.
deviation_anticipation: Executor may discover the 'emit both typed sink AND legacy log line' helper conflicts with existing logger.warning string-formatting (the chain_reconciliation field-label emissions use multi-line f-strings; a unifying helper must preserve the existing format string verbatim). Likely resolution: keep the existing logger.warning line untouched and add a side-effect-free counters.increment(...) call immediately after each. This is the conservative path; executor should not refactor the log strings.

Negative-env validation: the existing _db_busy_timeout_s catches malformed env via ValueError/TypeError and falls back to default 30000ms. T2F changes this to ValueError-at-parse-time for negative values specifically; positive-but-malformed strings should still fall back (preserving T1E behavior). Executor must not regress the malformed-string-falls-back-to-default path.
Doc cleanup may surface that phase.json files_touched drift is wider than 6 LOWs; if executor finds additional drift, surface it in BATCH_DONE rather than silently expand.
out_of_scope_explicit: Persistence of counter values to disk (a future T2-counter-persistence phase if needed; T2F sink is in-process only).
Wiring counters into Discord alert thresholds (T2E owns alert delivery; T2F only provides the read API).
Removing the 'logger.warning telemetry_counter event=' lines (deferred to a future deprecation phase once typed sink is the universal source-of-truth and dashboards have migrated).
rebuild_all_v2 partial-commit semantic CHANGE (T1E C-3) — only the operator-note documentation lands in T2F; the semantic itself is intentional per T1E close.
FOK/FAK fixture (T1G C-1) — promotion to fixture is deferred to follow-up phase if production telemetry shows usage; out of T2F scope per planner Round-3.

T1F case-sensitivity LOW (security probe-4: `legacy:` startswith case-sensitive on src/venue/polymarket_v2_adapter.py:319 / scripts/state_census.py placeholder detection) — AMD-T2-1: explicitly DEFERRED. The carry-forward is hypothetical (no production trigger observed; T1F security-reviewer flagged it as theoretical risk only). Defer pending production telemetry showing a placeholder envelope with case-mismatched 'Legacy:' / 'LEGACY:' prefix. T2F does NOT add case-folding; if production evidence emerges, a follow-up phase introduces case-insensitive matching across both surfaces consistently. Closing the audit trail between the s3 retrospective LOWs ledger row 'security probe-4 (T1F) | optional case-folding' and T2F scope: this is the explicit decision-of-record.
scope_amendments_anticipated: If executor finds the negative-env validation requires touching scripts/rebuild_calibration_pairs_v2.py (T1E surface) to apply consistently, that is a scope expansion — surface as deviation, do not silently expand.

batch_plan.B1: Typed sink primitive + 7 call-site call-through + caplog ledger + negative-env validation + census fail-closed + 5 phase.json doc cleanups in single atomic commit. If executor finds B1 LOC trending past 800, split into B1a (sink + call-throughs + ledger; ~500 LOC) and B1b (negative-env + census + doc cleanups; ~200 LOC); coordinator authorizes split via SendMessage.
